records.ety: [L. speculatus, p. p. of speculari to spy out, observe, fr. specula a lookout, fr. specere to look. See Spy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Speculated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Speculating.]

defs: To consider by turning a subject in the mind, and viewing it in its different aspects and relations; to meditate; to contemplate; to theorize; as, to speculate on questions in religion; to speculate on political events.
quotes: It is remarkable that persons who speculate the most boldly often conform with the most pefect quietude to the external regulations of society.

defs: To view subjects from certain premises given or assumed, and infer conclusions respecting them a priori.

To purchase with the expectation of a contingent advance in value, and a consequent sale at a profit; -- often, in a somewhat depreciative sense, of unsound or hazardous transactions; as, to speculate in coffee, in sugar, or in bank stock.
To consider attentively; as, to speculate the nature of a thing.
